mechanism: The vol-selling complex ($400-600B notional) creates structural vol compression during calm periods through gamma-pinning and systematic premium collection, but guarantees amplification during transitions as gamma hedging flips, VaR deleveraging triggers, and passive rebalancing creates cliff-risk.
regime_dependence: Amplification activates during regime transitions when dealer positioning flips. In calm markets the same complex suppresses vol. The relationship is directionally asymmetric — suppression is gradual, amplification is sudden.
